description: UI redesign sprint for the Plinth white-label AI agent platform: chat-first client experience, consultant dashboard overhaul, document store, onboarding wizard, and shared polish.

items.description: Initialize shadcn/ui, configure Tailwind v4 CSS-first in globals.css, and set up path aliases in tsconfig.json
steps: Run `npx shadcn@latest init` — components.json exists at project root with style=default, rsc=true, tsx=true, tailwind.css=src/app/globals.css, cssVariables=true, iconLibrary=lucide
src/lib/utils.ts exists and exports cn() utility function
tsconfig.json has path aliases: @/components, @/lib/utils, @/components/ui, @/lib, @/hooks
src/app/globals.css starts with `@import "tailwindcss";` — no tailwind.config.js or tailwind.config.ts exists at project root
npm run build completes with no Tailwind v4 configuration errors
npx tsc --noEmit passes with no path alias errors
items.notes: First item — all other foundation items depend on this. Use --legacy-peer-deps if npm install fails.

items.description: Define the full Plinth CSS variable palette (18 shadcn variables + Plinth-specific tokens + spacing + shadows) and dark theme stub in globals.css
steps: :root block defines all 18 shadcn variables: --primary (indigo #6366F1 as OKLCH), --primary-foreground (#FFFFFF), --secondary (#818CF8), --secondary-foreground (#1E1B4B), --accent (#10B981), --accent-foreground (#FFFFFF), --background (#F5F3FF), --foreground (#1E1B4B), --muted (#EDE9FE), --muted-foreground (#6B7280), --card (#FFFFFF), --card-foreground (#1E1B4B), --border (#E2E8F0), --input (#E2E8F0), --ring (#6366F1), --radius (0.5rem), --destructive (#EF4444), --destructive-foreground (#FFFFFF)
:root defines Plinth-specific tokens: --color-primary, --color-secondary, --color-cta, --color-background, --color-text
:root defines 7 spacing tokens (--space-xs through --space-3xl) and 4 shadow tokens (--shadow-sm through --shadow-xl)
[data-theme="dark"] block exists in globals.css (stubbed)
In browser devtools, --primary CSS variable resolves to an indigo color
items.notes: shadcn with Tailwind v4 uses OKLCH color space. Convert hex to OKLCH equivalents.

items.description: Load Plus Jakarta Sans via next/font/google, apply as default font in root layout, and install all 20 initial shadcn/ui components plus lucide-react
steps: src/app/layout.tsx imports Plus_Jakarta_Sans from next/font/google with subsets=["latin"], weight=["300","400","500","600","700"], variable="--font-plus-jakarta-sans", display="swap"
<html> has className={plusJakartaSans.variable}; <body> has className="font-sans antialiased bg-background text-foreground"
globals.css sets font-family: var(--font-plus-jakarta-sans), sans-serif on :root or body

src/components/ui/ contains all 20 component files: button, card, input, select, badge, dialog, sheet, tabs, avatar, dropdown-menu, skeleton, sidebar, breadcrumb, form, table, textarea, checkbox, separator, tooltip, sonner
lucide-react is in package.json dependencies; no emoji characters used as UI icons in src/ files
import './globals.css' is present in layout.tsx; layout exports RootLayout wrapping ConvexClientProvider and ThemeProvider
npm run build passes; browser shows Plus Jakarta Sans font and warm lavender (#F5F3FF) background
items.notes: The --font-plus-jakarta-sans CSS variable must be referenced via className on <html>, not imported in CSS.

items.description: Add conversations and messages tables to convex/schema.ts with all required fields and indexes
steps: conversations table has fields: tenantId, userId, agentConfigId (nullable), title, lastMessageAt, messageCount, platform, status, createdAt
conversations table has indexes: by_userId_lastMessageAt on [userId, lastMessageAt] and by_tenantId_userId on [tenantId, userId]

messages table has fields: conversationId, tenantId, role (union of user/assistant/system), content, streamingToken (nullable), isStreaming, agentConfigId (nullable), agentRunId (nullable), metadata (optional object), createdAt
messages table has indexes: by_conversationId on [conversationId] and by_conversationId_createdAt on [conversationId, createdAt]
npx tsc --noEmit passes with no type errors
items.notes: Foundation for all chat features. Must be completed before any conversations/messages API items.

items.description: Add documents table to convex/schema.ts with all required fields, types, and indexes
steps: documents table defined with fields: tenantId (Id<'tenants'>), userId (Id<'users'>), title (string), content (string | null), storageId (string | null), mimeType (string), source ('user' | 'agent'), agentRunId (Id<'agentRuns'> | null), agentConfigId (Id<'agentConfigs'> | null), googleDocUrl (string | null), wordCount (number | null), createdAt (number), updatedAt (number)
Three indexes: by_tenantId_createdAt on ['tenantId', 'createdAt'], by_tenantId_userId on ['tenantId', 'userId'], by_agentRunId on ['agentRunId']
npx tsc --noEmit passes with no schema errors

items.description: Add invitations table to convex/schema.ts and clerkOrgId field to consultants table
steps: consultants table now has clerkOrgId: v.optional(v.string()) field

invitations table exists with fields: tenantId, consultantId, email, displayName (v.union(v.string(), v.null())), clerkInvitationId, status (v.union of 'pending'|'accepted'|'revoked'|'expired'), sentAt, acceptedAt (v.union(v.number(), v.null())), createdAt
invitations has indexes: by_tenantId_status on ['tenantId', 'status'] and by_clerkInvitationId on ['clerkInvitationId']
npx tsc --noEmit passes with no type errors
items.notes: Depends on existing consultants schema from prior sprint

items.description: Install @convex-dev/persistent-text-streaming, @blocknote/core, @blocknote/mantine, mammoth, turndown, svix, @tanstack/react-table, react-hook-form, zod, react-markdown, remark-gfm packages and wire streaming component into convex.config.ts
steps: npm install @convex-dev/persistent-text-streaming @blocknote/core @blocknote/mantine @mantine/core @mantine/hooks mammoth turndown svix @tanstack/react-table react-hook-form @hookform/resolvers react-markdown remark-gfm --legacy-peer-deps exits 0
npm install --save-dev @types/mammoth @types/turndown vitest convex-test --legacy-peer-deps exits 0; package.json 'test' script updated to 'vitest run' (replaces 'echo No tests configured')
convex/convex.config.ts imports and registers the persistent-text-streaming component
npx convex dev --once (or equivalent type generation) shows no component errors
npx tsc --noEmit reports no missing module errors for any of these packages
items.notes: Consolidates package installs from chat-interface, document-store, and user-management specs. zod is already installed (v4) — no-op for that package. @mantine/core and @mantine/hooks are peer deps of @blocknote/mantine. @hookform/resolvers@^4 supports Zod v4.

items.description: Create Clerk middleware at src/middleware.ts with route protection, TEST_MODE bypass, and onboarding redirect logic for client users
steps: src/middleware.ts exports default clerkMiddleware; config.matcher excludes _next/static, _next/image, favicon.ico
Public routes: /, /sign-in(.*), /sign-up(.*), /api/webhooks/(.*), /oauth/(.*)
Non-public routes call auth.protect() — visiting /dashboard while unauthenticated redirects to /sign-in
When process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_TEST_MODE === 'true', auth checks are skipped entirely (use NEXT_PUBLIC_TEST_MODE, not TEST_MODE — must be accessible in Edge Runtime)
Client user hitting /app route (excluding /app/onboarding) with onboardingComplete absent/false: redirects 302 to /app/onboarding
Client user on /app/onboarding with onboardingComplete: true: redirects 302 to /app
Consultant users are not affected by onboarding redirect logic
npx tsc --noEmit passes
items.notes: Consolidates middleware from auth-and-routing and client-onboarding specs.

items.description: Create auth layout at src/app/(auth)/layout.tsx — centered full-screen container for sign-in/sign-up pages
steps: File src/app/(auth)/layout.tsx exists and exports a default React component
Layout renders a full-screen centered container with classes including min-h-screen, items-center, justify-center
bg-background Tailwind class is applied to the outer wrapper
Layout accepts and renders {children}
npx tsc --noEmit passes
items.notes: Depends on design-system-foundation for CSS variables.

items.description: Create root page (src/app/page.tsx) with role-based redirect: unauthenticated to /sign-in, consultants/admins to /dashboard, others to /app
steps: src/app/page.tsx is a server component using async/await
Calls await auth() from @clerk/nextjs/server to get userId
Unauthenticated users (userId null) redirected to /sign-in
Authenticated users with role 'consultant' or 'platform_admin' redirected to /dashboard
Authenticated users with any other role redirected to /app
npx tsc --noEmit passes

items.description: Implement conversations CRUD in convex/conversations.ts: listConversations, getConversation, createConversation — all with multi-tenancy enforcement
steps: listConversations calls requireAuth(), resolves userId from JWT, returns conversations ordered by lastMessageAt DESC with limit 20, scoped by caller's tenantId
A query with tenant A's JWT cannot return tenant B's conversations
getConversation accepts { conversationId }, calls requireAuth(), validates conversation.userId matches caller's userId
createConversation accepts optional { agentConfigId }, inserts conversation with tenantId/userId from JWT, returns Id<'conversations'>
npx tsc --noEmit passes
items.notes: Depends on conversations table in schema (Item 4).

items.description: Implement messages CRUD in convex/messages.ts: listMessages and createUserMessage with conversation ownership validation
steps: listMessages accepts { conversationId }, calls requireAuth(), validates caller owns the conversation, returns messages ordered by createdAt ASC
listMessages with a conversationId owned by a different user returns null or throws auth error
createUserMessage accepts { conversationId, content }, validates ownership, inserts message with role 'user', isStreaming false, tenantId from JWT
createUserMessage returns Id<'messages'>
npx tsc --noEmit passes
items.notes: Depends on messages table (Item 4) and conversations.ts for ownership check.

items.description: Implement POST /api/chat/stream HTTP action with SSE streaming via persistent-text-streaming and orchestrator routing
steps: convex/execution/chatStream.ts exports an HTTP action handler; route POST /api/chat/stream registered in convex/http.ts
Handler validates Clerk JWT from Authorization header — returns 401 for invalid token
Handler creates assistant message with isStreaming: true and streamingToken before streaming
Handler uses createTextStreamer from @convex-dev/persistent-text-streaming to write chunks
Response is text/event-stream content type with SSE chunks
On failure, assistant message content set to error message and isStreaming set to false
npx tsc --noEmit passes
items.notes: Depends on persistent-text-streaming package (Item 7), messages schema (Item 4).

items.description: Implement orchestratorRoute internal action that dynamically routes to sub-agents based on tenant's deployed agentConfigs
steps: convex/execution/orchestrator.ts exports internalAction named orchestratorRoute accepting { conversationId, userMessage, tenantId, userId }
Action queries tenant's deployed agentConfigs to build orchestrator system prompt dynamically — no hardcoded agent capabilities
If no agentConfigs deployed, returns 'Your consultant hasn't set up any agents yet.' without empty tool list
Uses claude-sonnet-4-6 via Agent SDK as primary execution mode
When user message names an agent, routes via tool call; when ambiguous, asks clarifying question; when no match, responds directly
npx tsc --noEmit passes
items.notes: Depends on agentConfigs table from prior sprint. Requires Agent SDK.

items.description: Implement document CRUD in convex/documents.ts: listDocuments, getDocument, createDocument, updateDocument, saveAgentDocument (internal)
steps: listDocuments query accepts optional sortBy ('createdAt'|'updatedAt'|'title'), calls requireAuth, scopes by tenantId, returns limit 50
getDocument query validates document.tenantId matches caller's tenantId; returns null on mismatch; includes signed URL if storageId is set
createDocument mutation sets tenantId/userId from JWT, mimeType 'text/markdown', createdAt/updatedAt to Date.now(), returns Id<'documents'>
updateDocument mutation validates tenant ownership, throws ConvexError on mismatch, always updates updatedAt
saveAgentDocument is internalMutation — idempotent per agentRunId (upserts via by_agentRunId index), sets source: 'agent'
npx tsc --noEmit passes
items.notes: Depends on documents table (Item 5).

items.description: Implement exportToGoogleDocs action and importFromDocx action in convex/documents.ts
steps: exportToGoogleDocs action accepts { documentId }, validates tenant ownership, calls createGoogleDoc from googleDocs.ts, updates googleDocUrl on document
exportToGoogleDocs throws recognizable error code (CREDENTIAL_EXPIRED or CREDENTIAL_MISSING) when Google credential is missing
importFromDocx action accepts { storageId, fileName }, downloads from Convex storage, converts via mammoth then turndown, creates document with source 'user'
importFromDocx returns the new Id<'documents'>
npx tsc --noEmit passes
items.notes: Depends on existing createGoogleDoc action in convex/integrations/googleDocs.ts.

items.description: Add provisionStarterAgent mutation to convex/agentConfigs.ts — idempotent per tenant, client-role only
steps: provisionStarterAgent checks for existing agentConfig with templateId matching 'general-assistant' for caller's tenant; creates one if absent
Second call returns same agentConfigId (idempotency)
Returns null when 'general-assistant' template does not exist
Throws authorization error when called by non-client role
Created agentConfig tenantId matches caller's tenantId from JWT
npx tsc --noEmit passes
items.notes: Depends on general-assistant seed data (Item 20).

items.description: Create completeOnboarding server action and onboarding redirect middleware logic
steps: src/app/(client)/app/onboarding/_actions.ts exists with 'use server' directive
completeOnboarding() calls clerkClient().users.updateUser with publicMetadata.onboardingComplete = true
Calling without authenticated session throws 'Unauthenticated' error
No publicMetadata updates exist in client-side code (grep for updateUser in .tsx outside _actions.ts returns no results)
items.notes: Middleware onboarding redirect logic is in Item 8.

items.description: Create convex/invitations.ts with createInvitation/updateInvitationStatus internal mutations and listInvitations query
steps: createInvitation is internalMutation accepting { tenantId, consultantId, email, displayName?, clerkInvitationId }, returns Id<'invitations'>
updateInvitationStatus is internalMutation accepting { clerkInvitationId, status, acceptedAt? }
listInvitations query calls requireAuth, verifies consultant ownership of tenantId, returns invitations ordered by sentAt DESC
Calling listInvitations with a tenantId belonging to a different consultant returns empty or throws authorization error
npx tsc --noEmit passes
items.notes: Depends on invitations table in schema (Item 6).

items.description: Add missing backend mutations, expand existing mutations, update validators, and seed data for UI sprint prerequisites
steps: convex/coachingCallReports.ts exports a markReviewed mutation that accepts { reportId }, calls requireAuth, validates consultant ownership, sets status to 'reviewed' and updatedAt to Date.now()

convex/tenants.ts exports an updateTenant mutation that accepts { tenantId, businessName?, ownerName?, ownerEmail?, website?, vertical?, notes? }, calls requireAuth, validates consultant ownership of tenant, patches fields and sets updatedAt

convex/consultants.ts updateConsultant mutation expanded to accept { displayName?, businessName?, supportEmail? } — currently only accepts displayName. Add businessName and supportEmail as v.optional(v.string()) args
convex/themes.ts VALID_FONT_FAMILIES updated to include 'Plus Jakarta Sans': ["Inter", "Poppins", "Lato", "Montserrat", "Open Sans", "Plus Jakarta Sans"]

Add v.literal('general') to the agentTemplates category union in convex/schema.ts. convex/seed.ts includes a 'general-assistant' agentTemplate entry with slug 'general-assistant', category 'general', executionMode 'autonomous', isActive true — seed is idempotent (checks for existing slug before insert)
Existing mutations are NOT modified: sendReportToCoach (line ~277) and markNoAction (line ~308) remain as-is
npx tsc --noEmit passes
items.notes: BLOCKER-1 fix: markReviewed was missing. updateTenant was missing. updateConsultant needs businessName/supportEmail fields for Settings Profile tab. VALID_FONT_FAMILIES needs Plus Jakarta Sans. general-assistant seed needed for provisionStarterAgent (Item 17).

items.description: Create server actions for inviteUser, resendInvitation, and revokeInvitation at src/app/(consultant)/clients/[tenantId]/_actions.ts
steps: File src/app/(consultant)/clients/[tenantId]/_actions.ts exists with 'use server' directive

inviteUser server action validates email with Zod z.string().email(), checks for existing user, retrieves clerkOrgId — returns { success: false, error } for invalid email, missing clerkOrgId, or existing user
inviteUser calls clerkClient().organizations.createOrganizationInvitation with role 'org:client' and saves record via createInvitation internal mutation
revokeInvitation calls Clerk revoke API then updateInvitationStatus with 'revoked'
resendInvitation revokes old, creates new, updates clerkInvitationId and sentAt
npx tsc --noEmit passes
items.notes: Depends on convex/invitations.ts (Item 19). Clerk SDK must be installed.

items.description: Create design system visual reference page at /design-system (dev-only) displaying all installed shadcn components with Plinth tokens
items.userStory: As a developer, when I navigate to /design-system in development mode, I see all shadcn components rendered with Plinth's color palette, typography, and spacing tokens.
steps: http://localhost:3000/design-system renders without 404 or errors
Page displays Button components in all variants (default, secondary, destructive, outline, ghost)
Card component has correct --card background (#FFFFFF) and --border color (#E2E8F0)
Badge, Input, Select, Checkbox, and Textarea components are visible and styled with Plinth tokens
All text renders in Plus Jakarta Sans
Page returns 404 or redirects in production build (NODE_ENV=production)
items.notes: Developer reference page only, not user-facing.

items.description: Create sign-in and sign-up pages with Clerk components styled with Plinth brand
items.userStory: As an unauthenticated visitor, when I navigate to /sign-in or /sign-up, I see centered Clerk forms styled with Plinth brand colors and Plus Jakarta Sans font.
steps: src/app/(auth)/sign-in/[[...sign-in]]/page.tsx renders <SignIn /> with appearance.variables.colorPrimary='#6366F1' and fontFamily='var(--font-plus-jakarta-sans)'; afterSignInUrl='/'
src/app/(auth)/sign-up/[[...sign-up]]/page.tsx renders <SignUp /> with matching brand appearance; afterSignUpUrl='/'
Both use catch-all route segments so Clerk sub-routes resolve correctly
Navigating to /sign-in and /sign-up while unauthenticated renders the Clerk widgets
npx tsc --noEmit passes
items.notes: Consolidated sign-in and sign-up into one item.

items.description: Add role guard to consultant layout and wire UserButton in both consultant and client layouts
items.userStory: As a consultant, I see a UserButton for profile/sign-out; as a client trying to access /dashboard, I am redirected to /app.
steps: Consultant layout calls await auth() — if role is 'client' or absent, redirects to /app
/dashboard as consultant/admin renders without redirect
Consultant layout renders <UserButton afterSignOutUrl='/sign-in' /> in sidebar footer or header
Client layout renders <UserButton afterSignOutUrl='/sign-in' /> in sidebar footer
Clicking Sign out from either layout redirects to /sign-in
npx tsc --noEmit passes
items.notes: Depends on consultant and client layouts existing.

items.description: Build custom 404 page, root error boundary, and route-group error boundaries
items.userStory: As a user who navigates to a non-existent URL or encounters a runtime error, I see a friendly page with recovery options.
steps: src/app/not-found.tsx renders '404', h1 'Page not found', and 'Go home' button linking to /
src/app/error.tsx has 'use client' directive, accepts error and reset props, renders Try again (calls reset) and Go home buttons
src/app/(consultant)/error.tsx and src/app/(client)/app/error.tsx both exist with matching error boundary contract
Navigating to /nonexistent-page renders the 404 component
npx tsc --noEmit passes
items.notes: Consolidated root + route-group error boundaries into one item.

items.description: Install and configure Sonner toast, mount <Toaster /> once in root layout, and create SkipToContent accessibility component
items.userStory: As a user performing actions, I see toast notifications; as a keyboard user, I can skip to main content with Tab+Enter.
steps: <Toaster position='bottom-right' richColors /> rendered once in src/app/layout.tsx body — grep '<Toaster' returns exactly one result
toast.success('Test') shows green toast in bottom-right; toast.error('Test') shows red toast
src/components/SkipToContent.tsx renders anchor with href='#main-content', sr-only classes that become visible on focus
<SkipToContent /> is first child inside <body> in layout.tsx
All <main> elements have id='main-content' and tabIndex={-1}
npx tsc --noEmit passes
items.notes: Toaster must NOT be duplicated in route-group layouts.

items.description: Add prefers-reduced-motion CSS, touch target minimums, and create reusable PageBreadcrumb component
items.userStory: As a user who prefers reduced motion, all animations are instant; as a mobile user, all touch targets are at least 44x44px.
steps: globals.css contains @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block setting animation-duration/transition-duration to 0.01ms on *, *::before, *::after
Interactive elements (Button, Link) achieve 44x44px minimum via min-h-11 min-w-11 or padding
src/components/PageBreadcrumb.tsx accepts items: Array<{ label: string; href?: string }>, renders shadcn Breadcrumb components
Labels > 40 chars truncated with '...' and full label in title attribute
Verify WCAG AA contrast ratios: foreground #1E1B4B on background #F5F3FF >= 7:1 (actual ~8.5:1); primary-foreground #FFFFFF on primary #6366F1 >= 4.5:1
npm run build passes
items.notes: OMISSION fix: added WCAG AA contrast ratio verification step.

items.description: Create route-group loading fallbacks and LoadingPage shared component with skeleton layouts
items.userStory: As a user navigating pages, I see skeleton placeholders during data fetch instead of blank screens.
steps: src/app/(consultant)/loading.tsx renders Skeleton for page title, 4 stat cards grid, and table area
src/app/(client)/app/loading.tsx renders 3 message bubble skeletons and input area skeleton
src/components/LoadingPage.tsx accepts optional variant prop ('dashboard'|'list'|'detail') with generic default
Both loading files use Skeleton from @/components/ui/skeleton
npx tsc --noEmit passes

items.description: Build ChatInterface root layout with sidebar + main area, ConversationSidebar with New Chat, agent list, and recent conversations
items.userStory: As a client, when I navigate to /app, I see a chat layout with a 260px sidebar showing agents and recent conversations, and a main chat area.
steps: src/components/chat/ChatInterface.tsx renders full-screen flex layout; sidebar is 260px on desktop (>= 768px), hidden on mobile with Sheet hamburger toggle
Main chat area fills remaining space (flex-1); <main> element has id='main-content' and tabIndex={-1}
src/components/chat/ConversationSidebar.tsx has '+ New Chat' button calling createConversation and navigating to /app/{conversationId}
AGENTS section renders each deployed agentConfig as clickable item creating a conversation
RECENT section renders up to 20 conversations ordered by lastMessageAt DESC via listConversations query
Active conversation highlighted with bg-muted; sidebar can collapse to icon-only on desktop
npm run build passes
items.notes: Depends on conversations functional items (Items 11-12). OMISSION fix: id='main-content' and tabIndex={-1} on main element. WARNING: src/app/(client)/app/layout.tsx already exists as a 'use client' component with old nav items (Integrations, not Connections; no Documents). This layout must be fully replaced with the new client sidebar. Coordinate with Items 33 (Connections rename) and 45 (Documents nav).

items.description: Build MessageList, MessageBubble, AgentAvatar, TypingIndicator, EmptyState, and MessageInput chat components
items.userStory: As a client, I see my messages right-aligned in indigo bubbles, agent messages left-aligned with avatars and markdown rendering, and I can type/send messages with Enter.
steps: MessageList.tsx renders scrollable list of MessageBubble components, auto-scrolls on new messages
User messages: right-aligned, bg-primary text-primary-foreground, rounded-2xl rounded-br-sm, max-width 75%
Agent messages: left-aligned, bg-card border, AgentAvatar 16x16px, max-width 85%, markdown via react-markdown with remark-gfm (bold, italic, lists, code blocks render correctly)
TypingIndicator.tsx renders animated three-dot indicator while isStreaming: true on latest assistant message
EmptyState.tsx renders welcome message and 3-4 suggested prompts when no conversation is active
MessageInput.tsx: textarea auto-grows to 120px then scrolls; Enter sends, Shift+Enter newlines; send button disabled when empty or streaming
Sending calls createUserMessage then triggers POST /api/chat/stream
npm run build passes
items.notes: Depends on design-system-foundation for react-markdown.

items.description: Wire up /app and /app/[conversationId] pages to ChatInterface, handle routing edge cases
items.userStory: As a client, when I navigate to /app I see the empty state, and when I navigate to a conversation URL I see that conversation's messages.
steps: src/app/(client)/app/page.tsx renders ChatInterface with EmptyState
src/app/(client)/app/[conversationId]/page.tsx renders ChatInterface with specified conversation loaded
Navigating to /app/{invalidId} redirects to /app with toast 'Conversation not found'
Navigating to /app/{conversationId} owned by different user redirects to /app
ArtifactPanel at src/components/chat/ArtifactPanel.tsx renders as right-side panel for agent output documents
npm run build passes
items.notes: Depends on auth-and-routing for client route protection.

items.description: Build OnboardingWizard with 3 steps (Welcome, WhatYouCanDo, Ready) and wire to /app/onboarding page
items.userStory: As a new client, when I arrive at /app/onboarding, I step through a 3-step wizard in a centered card, and clicking 'Start chatting' provisions my starter agent and takes me to the chat interface.
steps: OnboardingWizard.tsx is 'use client' component managing step state (1-3); card is bg-card rounded-2xl shadow-lg p-8 max-w-[480px] centered; progress indicator shows 3 dots filled by --color-primary
Step 1: heading 'Welcome to {platformName}' (fallback 'Plinth'), 'Let's go' CTA (bg-accent), 'Skip setup' link — skip calls completeOnboarding() and redirects to /app
Step 2: heading 'Here's what your AI assistant can do', 3 bullet points, 'Next' CTA, 'Back' link, 'Skip setup' link
Step 3: heading 'You're all set!', 'Start chatting' CTA triggers provisionStarterAgent then completeOnboarding() then redirects to /app; no skip link; toast error on failure
src/app/(client)/app/onboarding/page.tsx renders OnboardingWizard, does NOT render standard app sidebar
npx tsc --noEmit passes
items.notes: Depends on provisionStarterAgent (Item 17) and completeOnboarding (Item 18). IMPORTANT: The onboarding page at src/app/(client)/app/onboarding/page.tsx inherits the client app layout sidebar. To suppress the sidebar, create a sub-layout at src/app/(client)/app/onboarding/layout.tsx that renders only {children} without the sidebar wrapper, OR use a route group like (client)/app/(no-sidebar)/onboarding/.

items.description: Rename Integrations page to Connections with 301 redirect, update sidebar nav and all copy
items.userStory: As a client, 'Connections' replaces 'Integrations' in the sidebar; the old URL redirects; page copy uses friendly language.
steps: Sidebar nav reads 'Connections' not 'Integrations'
GET /app/connections returns 200; GET /app/integrations returns 301 redirect to /app/connections
Page heading: 'Link your apps'; subtitle: 'Connect the tools your agents need to work their magic.'
Section heading: 'Connected apps'; Connect button: 'Link account'; Disconnect button: 'Unlink'
Empty state: 'No apps needed yet. Your consultant will let you know when you need to link something.'
Status badges use Badge variant='default' (emerald) for connected, variant='secondary' for unconnected

items.description: Build consultant sidebar layout with SidebarProvider, collapsible nav, Sheet drawer on mobile, and Clerk UserButton
items.userStory: As a consultant, when I open any consultant page on mobile, I see a hamburger button; clicking it opens the navigation drawer with all nav items.
steps: Consultant layout at src/app/(consultant)/layout.tsx wraps in <SidebarProvider>; <main> element has id='main-content' and tabIndex={-1}
ConsultantSidebar has nav items: Dashboard, Clients, Agents, Reports, Settings with Lucide icons
Sidebar header shows Plinth wordmark/logo; footer shows <UserButton afterSignOutUrl='/sign-in' /> and consultant name
Click SidebarTrigger on desktop — collapses to icon-only; click again — expands
At < 768px, sidebar hidden; hamburger trigger (min 44x44px) opens Sheet drawer
Active nav item has bg-sidebar-accent and aria-current='page'
<nav> has role='navigation' and aria-label='Consultant navigation'
items.notes: WARNING: src/app/(consultant)/layout.tsx already exists with an inline nav bar from Cycle 6. Read the existing file first, then replace entirely with SidebarProvider pattern. Do not append to the existing layout. Files: src/app/(consultant)/layout.tsx, src/components/consultant/ConsultantSidebar.tsx. OMISSION fix: id='main-content' and tabIndex={-1} on main element.

items.description: Build consultant dashboard page with time-aware greeting, 4 stat cards, recent clients table, and recent draft reports
items.userStory: As a consultant, when I navigate to the dashboard, I see my 4 key stats and recent clients without horizontal scrolling.
steps: Greeting reads 'Good morning/afternoon/evening, {firstName}' based on time of day
4 stat cards (Total clients, Active agents, Runs this month, Cost this month) in responsive grid: 4-col desktop, 2-col tablet, stacked mobile
Recent clients table: max 5 rows with Client name, Status badge, Agents count, Last run date; 'View all clients' link to /clients
Draft reports section: max 3 reports with client name, score, and 'Review' button
Skeleton loading states for stat cards and clients table; empty state card with 'Add your first client' CTA when no clients exist
No horizontal scroll at 375px viewport
items.notes: Depends on ConsultantSidebar (Item 34). Use existing queries: api.dashboard.getConsultantDashboard (stat data) and api.dashboard.listClientsForConsultant (recent clients table). Do NOT create new queries.

items.description: Build clients list page with TanStack Table (6 columns, search, status filter, pagination, kebab menu) and empty state with Add Client dialog
items.userStory: As a consultant, when I click a column header the table sorts, and when I type in the search field the table filters in real-time.
steps: TanStack Table with columns: Business name (link), Owner, Status (badge), Agents (count), Last run (relative date), Actions
Click column header — sorts ascending then descending; aria-sort attribute updates
Search input filters client-side without page reload; status Select filters by paused/active/all
25 rows per page with pagination controls; 'View' navigates to /clients/{tenantId}
Kebab menu shows Pause, Edit, Archive; table has <caption> and <th scope='col'>
Empty state: 'No clients yet' with 'Add client' button opening Dialog form
At 375px, table renders as stacked cards or is horizontally scrollable
items.notes: Requires @tanstack/react-table.

items.description: Build client detail page shell: breadcrumb, heading with status badge, 4-tab container (Agent Configs, Run History, Reports, Client Settings)
items.userStory: As a consultant, when I open a client detail page, I see tabbed content and can switch between tabs.
steps: Breadcrumb reads 'Clients / {businessName}'; heading shows businessName with status badge
4 tabs render: Agent Configs, Run History, Reports, Client Settings — Users tab added separately in Item 48
Clicking each tab switches content without page reload; skeleton loading per tab
ConsultantSidebar is present with 'Clients' highlighted as active
Convex query scopes by consultantId — consultant cannot access another consultant's client
npx tsc --noEmit passes
items.notes: BLOCKER-2 fix: 4 tabs initially; Users tab wired as 5th tab by Item 48. Tab content in Items 38, 39.

items.description: Build client detail tab content: Agent Configs tab (table + deploy dialog) and Run History tab (table + agent filter)
items.userStory: As a consultant on the Agent Configs tab, I can see deployed agents and deploy new ones from the template library.
steps: Agent Configs tab: table with Name, Status, Template, Deployed, Version, Actions (Configure/Pause/Deploy); 'Add agent config' opens Dialog with template select; empty state 'Deploy your first agent'
Run History tab: table with Agent, Status, Triggered, Duration, Cost, Actions; 'View' navigates to run detail; agent dropdown filter
When no agent configs exist, tab shows empty state: 'Deploy your first agent' button
When no runs exist, tab shows empty state message
items.notes: Depends on client detail page shell (Item 37). Split from original combined tab content item.

items.description: Build client detail tab content: Reports tab (table) and Client Settings tab (Zod-validated form calling updateTenant)
items.userStory: As a consultant on the Reports tab, I can see reports; on Settings tab, I can edit client details with validation.
steps: Reports tab: table with Date, Coach, Score, Status, Actions; 'Review' navigates to report detail; scoped to tenantId
Client Settings tab: editable fields (Business name, Owner name, Owner email, Website, Vertical select, Notes) + read-only (Consultant ID, Tenant ID, Created date)
Submit with empty Business name shows Zod error 'Business name is required' inline
Submit with valid data calls updateTenant mutation (Item 20); success toast confirms save
items.notes: Depends on client detail page shell (Item 37) and updateTenant mutation (Item 20). Split from original combined tab content item.

items.description: Build agent templates library page with category filter tabs and responsive card grid with 'Deploy to client' dialog
items.userStory: As a consultant, when I click 'Deploy to client' on a template card, a dialog opens to select a client and confirm deployment.
steps: Heading 'Agent Library' with category tabs: All, Marketing, Sales, Operations, Coaching
Card grid: 3 columns desktop, 2 tablet, 1 mobile; TemplateCard shows name, description (2 lines truncated), category badge, Active/Pipeline badges

'Deploy to client' button opens Dialog with client Select and display name input; confirm calls deployAgentConfig (NOT createAgentConfig — use the existing mutation name); toast 'Agent deployed to {clientName}'
Templates fetched via Convex agentTemplates query; skeleton cards during loading

items.description: Build reports list page with TanStack Table (8 columns, status/score/flagged filters, color-coded score badges)
items.userStory: As a consultant, when I filter by 'Low' score range, only reports with scores below 70 remain.
steps: Heading 'Reports' with count badge; TanStack Table columns: Date, Client, Coach, Student, Call #, Score (badge), Status (badge), Actions
Score badges: >= 80 emerald, 70-79 amber, < 70 red; Status badges: draft muted, reviewed blue, sent green, no_action gray
Status Select, Score range Select ('Low < 70'), 'Show flagged only' checkbox filters
Column headers sortable with aria-sort; table has <caption> and <th scope='col'>; badges have role='status'
Skeleton rows during loading

items.description: Build report detail page with two-panel layout: scores/metadata on left, editable narrative + action buttons on right
items.userStory: As a consultant, I can read the narrative, edit it, release it to the coach, and mark it as reviewed.
steps: Breadcrumb 'Reports / {coachName} - Call {callNumber}'; left panel (1/3): overall score (colored by tier), dimension scorecards, call metadata, flagged badge
Right panel (2/3): narrative in Textarea (pre-filled editedNarrative or narrative); '(edited)' badge when editedNarrative set
'Save edits' calls updateReportNarrative (original not overwritten); 'Mark Reviewed' calls markReviewed mutation (Item 20)
'Release to Coach' calls sendReportToCoach mutation; 'Mark No Action' calls markNoAction mutation
At 375px, panels stack vertically; 'No dimension scores available' when no scores exist; skeleton during loading
items.notes: BLOCKER-1 fix: uses actual mutation names — markReviewed (Item 20), sendReportToCoach, markNoAction.

items.description: Build settings page with Profile, Theme, and Billing tabs — including hex color validation, live preview, and logo upload
items.userStory: As a consultant, when I update my primary color in Theme settings, I see a live preview before saving.
steps: 3 tabs: Profile, Theme, Billing
Profile: Display name (required), Business name (required), Email (read-only), Support email; Zod validation; save calls updateConsultant; success toast
Theme: Platform name, 5 hex color inputs with swatch preview; '#xyz' shows 'Invalid hex color format' inline; save disabled while invalid

Theme live preview panel updates when valid colors entered; logo upload via Convex generateUploadUrl; 'Save theme' calls upsertTheme (NOT updateTheme — use the existing mutation name); font family field is a Select with valid values from VALID_FONT_FAMILIES: Inter, Poppins, Lato, Montserrat, Open Sans, Plus Jakarta Sans
Billing: read-only plan info + 'Upgrade plan' external link
Tab switching does not reset unsaved form state
items.notes: All color inputs must validate #XXXXXX per CLAUDE.md constraint.

items.description: Build run detail page for consultant: run header, step timeline with status icons, and output section with Google Doc links
items.userStory: As a consultant, when I view a run detail page, I see the step-by-step timeline with status icons and final output.
steps: Breadcrumb 'Clients / {clientName} / Runs / {runId short}'; header card: agent name, status badge, trigger type, start time, duration, cost
Step timeline renders vertically with one Card per step: pending (muted), running (Loader2 spinner), completed (green CheckCircle2), failed (red XCircle + error message)
Output section renders key-value display; Google Doc URLs as clickable links with ExternalLink icon
Skeleton cards during loading

items.description: Add 'Documents' nav item to client sidebar, build document list page with sort controls, DocumentList table, and EmptyDocuments state
items.userStory: As a client, when I navigate to /app/documents, I see my documents in a table with sort options, or an empty state with a 'Create document' button.
steps: Client sidebar includes { href: '/app/documents', label: 'Documents', icon: 'FileText' } between Agents and Connections; active state highlights on /app/documents and /app/documents/[id]
Page heading 'Documents', 'New document' button (bg-accent), Sort By Select (Date created / Date modified / Name)
useQuery(api.documents.listDocuments, { sortBy }) updates list on sort change
'New document' calls createDocument({title: 'Untitled document'}) then navigates to /app/documents/[newId]
DocumentList.tsx: shadcn Table with Title (link), Source badge ('You' or agent name), Date (relative), Actions (Open + kebab: Rename/Export/Delete)
EmptyDocuments renders 'No documents yet' message with 'Create document' button when list is empty
items.notes: Split from original combined document UI item. Covers list page and table only.

items.description: Build DocumentEditor (BlockNote with auto-save), DocumentToolbar (editable title, export, import), and document editor page
items.userStory: As a client, when I open a document, I see a breadcrumb, toolbar with title/export/import, and a full-height BlockNote editor that auto-saves.
steps: DocumentEditor.tsx: 'use client', BlockNote initialized with markdownToBlocks; changes debounce 1000ms then call updateDocument; shows 'Saving...' then 'Saved'
Navigation guard: shadcn Dialog 'You have unsaved changes. Leave anyway?' with Confirm/Cancel
DocumentToolbar.tsx: editable title (blur saves), Export to Google Docs (loading state, toast on success, credential error handling), Import .docx (file picker, accept='.docx' only)
Import flow: file upload to Convex storage, importFromDocx call, redirect to new document; non-.docx file shows error toast

Editor page at src/app/(client)/app/documents/[documentId]/page.tsx: breadcrumb 'Documents / {title}', full-height editor; 'Document not found' for invalid IDs; fallback raw markdown in <pre> if BlockNote parse fails
items.notes: Split from original combined document UI item. Covers editor, toolbar, and editor page.

items.description: Build UsersTab for client detail page: InviteUserForm dialog, InvitationsTable with resend/revoke, ActiveUsersTable
items.userStory: As a consultant on the Users tab, I see active users and pending invitations, and can invite new users via a dialog form.
steps: InviteUserForm: email (required, Zod validated), display name (optional), disabled role select showing 'Client'; Loader2 spinner on submit; toast 'Invitation sent to {email}'; inline error on failure
InvitationsTable: columns Email, Sent (relative date), Status (Badge: Pending yellow, Expired gray, Revoked red), Actions; 'Resend' calls resendInvitation; 'Revoke' opens AlertDialog confirmation
ActiveUsersTable: columns Name, Email, Role, Joined, Actions; 'Remove user' disabled (v1)
When clerkOrgId not set on consultant, tab shows 'Organization not configured. Contact support.'
items.notes: Depends on invitation server actions (Item 21) and client detail page shell (Item 37). Components only — wiring as tab in Item 48.

items.description: Wire UsersTab as 5th tab on client detail page with count badge and Invite user button
items.userStory: As a consultant, when I click the 'Users' tab on a client detail page, I see active users and pending invitations.
steps: UsersTab composes ActiveUsersTable, InvitationsTable, and InviteUserForm components from Item 47
'Invite user' Button positioned at top-right of the tab; clicking opens Dialog containing InviteUserForm
Tab label shows count of active users + pending invitations: e.g. 'Users (3)'
Users tab appears as 5th tab after Agent Configs, Run History, Reports, Settings on client detail page (Item 37)
npx tsc --noEmit passes
items.notes: Depends on UsersTab components (Item 47) and client detail page shell (Item 37). Adds the 5th tab.

items.description: Wire agentWorkflow.ts to call saveAgentDocument when agent output contains document-type content
steps: Read the existing convex/execution/agentWorkflow.ts file to understand current structure before making changes
agentWorkflow.ts checks agent output for type: 'document' or outputType: 'document'
When detected, calls internal('documents:saveAgentDocument') with tenantId, agentRunId, agentConfigId, title, content
A document with source: 'agent' appears in documents table linked to agentRunId
npx tsc --noEmit passes
items.notes: Depends on saveAgentDocument (Item 15). OMISSION fix: added step to read existing file before modifying.

items.description: Create Clerk webhook handler for organizationMembership.created and organizationInvitation.expired events, register in http.ts
steps: convex/webhooks/clerk.ts verifies svix-id, svix-timestamp, svix-signature headers — returns 400 for invalid

organizationMembership.created: resolves consultantId from clerkOrgId, resolves tenantId from invitation by email, creates users record (idempotent by clerkUserId), sets Clerk publicMetadata { role: 'client', tenantId: string }
Updates invitation status to 'accepted'; if no invitation found, creates user with tenantId: null
organizationInvitation.expired: updates invitation status to 'expired' via by_clerkInvitationId index
POST /webhooks/clerk route registered in convex/http.ts
npx tsc --noEmit passes
items.notes: Depends on invitations.ts (Item 19); svix package (Item 7). IMPORTANT: The existing createUser mutation in users.ts requires platform_admin role — the webhook handler MUST use an internalMutation for user creation (create a new createUserInternal in users.ts), NOT the existing createUser. CLERK_WEBHOOK_SECRET and CLERK_SECRET_KEY must both be set in Convex env vars.

items.description: Unit/integration tests for conversations.ts and messages.ts: CRUD operations and multi-tenancy enforcement
steps: createConversation with valid client JWT inserts conversation with correct tenantId/userId from JWT
listConversations with client A's JWT does not return client B's conversations
getConversation with conversationId owned by different user returns null or throws
createUserMessage with conversationId owned by different user throws authorization error
listMessages with conversationId owned by different user throws authorization error
createUserMessage sets role 'user', isStreaming false, tenantId from JWT
npm test passes
items.notes: Multi-tenancy enforcement is non-negotiable.

items.description: Unit/integration tests for documents.ts: listDocuments, getDocument, createDocument, updateDocument, saveAgentDocument — tenant isolation and idempotency
steps: listDocuments: tenant A cannot see tenant B's documents; sortBy 'createdAt' returns newest first; sortBy 'title' returns alphabetical; max 50 returned
getDocument: valid doc returns document; wrong tenant returns null; doc with storageId includes signedUrl; non-existent returns null
createDocument: sets tenantId/userId from JWT, mimeType 'text/markdown', sets createdAt/updatedAt
updateDocument: updates fields + updatedAt; throws for wrong tenant
saveAgentDocument: first call inserts with source 'agent'; second call with same agentRunId updates (no duplicate)
npm test passes
items.notes: Consolidates 4 test-backend items from document-store spec.

items.description: Unit/integration tests for exportToGoogleDocs, importFromDocx, provisionStarterAgent, and completeOnboarding
steps: exportToGoogleDocs: valid doc + credential calls createGoogleDoc and updates googleDocUrl; missing credential throws CREDENTIAL_MISSING
importFromDocx: valid .docx storageId creates document with source 'user' and markdown content; returns new documentId
provisionStarterAgent: creates agentConfig for client; second call returns same ID; missing template returns null; non-client throws
completeOnboarding: no session throws; valid session calls clerkClient updateUser with onboardingComplete: true
npm test passes
items.notes: Consolidates document action tests with onboarding backend tests.

items.description: Unit/integration tests for invitations.ts and invitation server actions: CRUD, validation, multi-tenancy, and Clerk API integration
steps: createInvitation inserts and returns Id; updateInvitationStatus updates matching record; non-existent clerkInvitationId does not throw
listInvitations returns only invitations for specified tenantId ordered by sentAt DESC; different consultant gets auth error
inviteUser: invalid email returns error; no clerkOrgId returns 'Organization not configured'; existing user returns 'already has account'
revokeInvitation calls Clerk revoke + updates status to 'revoked'
resendInvitation revokes old, creates new, updates clerkInvitationId and sentAt
npm test passes
items.notes: Mock Clerk client for testing.

items.description: Backend tests for Clerk webhook handler: signature verification, event handling, idempotency
steps: Request without valid svix headers returns HTTP 400
organizationMembership.created creates user with correct tenantId, consultantId, role; duplicate clerkUserId does not create second record
organizationMembership.created with no matching invitation creates user with tenantId: null
organizationInvitation.expired updates invitation status to 'expired'
npm test passes

items.description: E2E tests for design system foundation: font loading, CSS variables, and component rendering on /design-system page
items.userStory: As a developer, when I run E2E tests, design system foundation tests confirm fonts load, CSS variables resolve, and shadcn components render with Plinth styles.
steps: Test file at tests/e2e/design-system.spec.ts
Test: navigate to /, verify --font-plus-jakarta-sans CSS variable is non-empty
Test: verify --primary and --background CSS variables are defined
Test: navigate to /design-system, heading is visible with no console errors
Test: Button on /design-system has cursor-pointer computed style
npx playwright test passes

items.description: E2E tests for auth flows: unauthenticated redirects, role-based redirects after sign-in, cross-role guard, and sign-out
items.userStory: As any user, auth redirects work correctly: unauthenticated to /sign-in, consultants to /dashboard, clients to /app, cross-role blocked.
steps: Test: navigate to / without session — URL contains /sign-in; /dashboard without session — /sign-in; /app without session — /sign-in
Test: /sign-in renders Clerk sign-in form
Test: sign in as consultant — URL is /dashboard; as client — URL is /app; as platform_admin — /dashboard; as no-role — /app
Test: authenticated client navigates to /dashboard — redirected to /app; page does NOT show consultant UI
Test: consultant clicks UserButton Sign out — URL is /sign-in; client clicks Sign out — URL is /sign-in; session cleared
npx playwright test passes
items.notes: Consolidated 4 auth E2E items into one.

items.description: E2E tests for chat interface: send message, streaming response, conversation history, sidebar navigation, error cases
items.userStory: As a client, when I type a message and press Enter, it sends and the agent streams a response; I can resume past conversations from the sidebar.
steps: Test: /app as client shows EmptyState with suggested prompts
Test: type message, press Enter — user bubble appears right-aligned; TypingIndicator shows during streaming; agent response renders with markdown
Test: textarea and send button disabled while streaming; send button disabled when empty
Test: conversation appears in sidebar; clicking it navigates to /app/{conversationId} with messages loaded
Test: '+ New Chat' creates conversation and navigates to new URL
Test: /app/invalid-id redirects to /app with 'Conversation not found' toast
npx playwright test passes

items.description: E2E tests for onboarding wizard: happy path (3 steps + redirect), skip flow, back navigation
items.userStory: As a new client, I complete onboarding or skip it, and I'm taken to the chat interface.
steps: Test: new client navigating to /app redirected to /app/onboarding; step counter shows 'Step 1 of 3'
Test: 'Let's go' advances to step 2; 'Next' to step 3; 'Start chatting' provisions agent and redirects to /app
Test: after redirect, /app/onboarding redirects back to /app
Test: 'Skip setup' on step 1 or 2 completes onboarding and redirects to /app; step 3 has no skip link
Test: 'Back' on step 2 returns to step 1; 'Back' on step 3 returns to step 2; step 1 has no 'Back'
npx playwright test passes
items.notes: Consolidated 3 onboarding E2E items.

items.description: E2E test for Connections page rename: redirect from old URL, updated copy
items.userStory: As a client, the old /app/integrations URL redirects to /app/connections with updated copy.
steps: Test: /app/integrations as authenticated client results in final URL /app/connections
Test: page heading 'Link your apps'; sidebar item 'Connections' not 'Integrations'
Test: empty state contains 'No apps needed yet'; connect button 'Link account'; disconnect button 'Unlink'
npx playwright test passes

items.description: E2E tests for document store: create, edit, auto-save, sort, empty state, unsaved changes guard
items.userStory: As a client, I can create, edit, and sort documents, with auto-save and unsaved changes protection.
steps: Test: /app/documents shows heading, 'New document' button; click creates doc and navigates to editor with breadcrumb 'Documents / Untitled document'
Test: click title, change to 'My Test Document', blur — breadcrumb updates; type in editor — 'Saving...' appears then 'Saved'
Test: with no documents, empty state shows 'No documents yet' with 'Create document' button
Test: sort controls change list order (Name alphabetical, Date created newest first, Date modified newest first)
Test: type in editor, click sidebar nav — unsaved changes dialog appears; Cancel stays, Leave navigates away
npx playwright test passes
items.notes: Consolidated 5 document E2E items.

items.description: E2E tests for consultant sidebar navigation and clients table interactions
items.userStory: As a consultant, sidebar navigation and clients table sorting/filtering/pagination all work correctly.
steps: Test: /dashboard renders sidebar with 5 nav items; clicking 'Clients' navigates to /clients with aria-current='page'
Test: SidebarTrigger collapses/expands on desktop; at 375px hamburger opens Sheet drawer
Test: /clients table has sortable columns with aria-sort; search filters in real-time; status filter works
Test: pagination with 25+ clients shows page 2; 'View' navigates to /clients/{tenantId}; kebab shows Pause/Edit/Archive
Test: unauthenticated /dashboard redirects to /sign-in
npx playwright test passes
items.notes: Consolidated sidebar + clients table E2E.

items.description: E2E tests for consultant settings and report detail page
items.userStory: As a consultant, settings form validation works and report detail editing/actions are reflected in the UI.
steps: Test: /settings Profile tab — empty Display name shows Zod error; valid data + Save shows success toast
Test: Theme tab — '#xyz' shows error, save disabled; '#FF5733' clears error, preview updates; Save theme shows toast
Test: Billing tab — read-only plan info; Upgrade link is anchor not button
Test: /reports/{reportId} — two-panel layout; edit narrative + Save shows '(edited)' badge; 'Mark Reviewed' updates badge
Test: at 375px panels stack vertically; report with no dimension scores shows placeholder text
npx playwright test passes
items.notes: Consolidated settings + report detail E2E.

items.description: E2E tests for user management: invite user, revoke invitation, and no-org error state
items.userStory: As a consultant, I can invite users, revoke invitations, and see an error when my org is not configured.
steps: Test: on Users tab, click 'Invite user' — Dialog opens; fill valid email, submit — toast 'Invitation sent to {email}'; email appears in Pending table
Test: invalid email shows inline error; no toast
Test: click 'Revoke' on pending invitation — AlertDialog appears; Cancel closes; Revoke removes/updates row
Test: consultant with no clerkOrgId sees 'Organization not configured. Contact support.' on Users tab; Invite button absent/disabled
npx playwright test passes
items.notes: Consolidated 3 user management E2E items.

items.description: E2E tests for shared polish: 404 page, skip-to-content accessibility, toast notifications, and responsive no-scroll check
items.userStory: As a user, the 404 page works, skip-to-content is keyboard accessible, toasts appear, and no page has horizontal scroll on mobile.
steps: Test: /this-page-does-not-exist renders '404' and 'Page not found'; 'Go home' navigates to /
Test: Tab from address bar lands on 'Skip to content'; Enter moves focus to #main-content
Test: toast.success shows visible toast; toast.error shows visible toast; auto-dismisses within 10s
Test: at 375x812 viewport, / and at least 2 other pages have document.body.scrollWidth <= clientWidth
npx playwright test passes
items.notes: Consolidated 4 shared polish E2E items.
